e_visa.notes: Online visitor visa applications can be submitted through the official ISS eServices portal at eservices.iss.gov.gy. Visa types include Visitor, Business, Employment, and Student. This is an online pre-approval system rather than a traditional e-visa; applicants receive a reference number to check status. The portal returned HTTP 403 on re-fetch June 2026 - the URL is known from prior validated data.

visa_policy.notes: Visa-free list sourced primarily from Guyana Ministry of Foreign Affairs (minfor.gov.gy/visa-information), re-verified June 2026. Secondary source: ISS eligible-countries page (eservices.iss.gov.gy/eligible-countries-landing-permit) and Embassy of Guyana Belgium (embassyofguyana.be). Guyana's immigration eServices portal (eservices.iss.gov.gy) handles online visa applications. Nationals of countries not on the visa-free list must apply for a visa at a Guyana embassy/consulate or, if no diplomatic presence is accessible, apply online for a visa approval letter (treated as visa-on-arrival upon presentation). Countries listed where only diplomatic/official/service passports qualify are noted in conditional_access, NOT visa_free.

conditional_access.conditions: Guatemalan nationals holding a US Permanent Resident Card may enter Guyana visa-free for up to 90 days. MFA verbatim: 'Guatemala for 90 days for holders of Diplomatic/Official Passports and holders of an US Permanent Resident Card or a valid US/Canadian/Schengen Visa'.

Guatemalan nationals holding a valid US visa may enter Guyana visa-free for up to 90 days. MFA verbatim: 'Guatemala for 90 days for holders of Diplomatic/Official Passports and holders of an US Permanent Resident Card or a valid US/Canadian/Schengen Visa'.

Guatemalan nationals holding a valid Canadian visa may enter Guyana visa-free for up to 90 days. MFA verbatim: 'Guatemala for 90 days for holders of Diplomatic/Official Passports and holders of an US Permanent Resident Card or a valid US/Canadian/Schengen Visa'.

Guatemalan nationals holding a valid Schengen visa may enter Guyana visa-free for up to 90 days. MFA verbatim: 'Guatemala for 90 days for holders of Diplomatic/Official Passports and holders of an US Permanent Resident Card or a valid US/Canadian/Schengen Visa'.

Honduran nationals holding a US Permanent Resident Card may enter Guyana visa-free for up to 90 days. Source: Embassy of Guyana Belgium and ISS eligible-countries page verbatim: '90 days holders of a US Permanent Resident Card or a valid US/Canadian/Schengen Visa'. NOT independently confirmed on minfor.gov.gy - Honduras does not appear in the enumerated MFA list.

Honduran nationals holding a valid US visa may enter Guyana visa-free for up to 90 days. Source: Embassy of Guyana Belgium and ISS eligible-countries page. NOT independently confirmed on minfor.gov.gy.

Honduran nationals holding a valid Canadian visa may enter Guyana visa-free for up to 90 days. Source: Embassy of Guyana Belgium and ISS eligible-countries page. NOT independently confirmed on minfor.gov.gy.

Honduran nationals holding a valid Schengen visa may enter Guyana visa-free for up to 90 days. Source: Embassy of Guyana Belgium and ISS eligible-countries page. NOT independently confirmed on minfor.gov.gy.

Mexican ordinary passport holders with a US Permanent Resident Card may enter visa-free for up to 90 days. Source: Embassy of Guyana Belgium and ISS eligible-countries page. Note: minfor.gov.gy lists Mexico only for diplomatic/official/service passport holders - ordinary-passport credential access is NOT confirmed on the primary MFA source.
Mexican ordinary passport holders with a valid US visa may enter visa-free for up to 90 days. Source: Embassy of Guyana Belgium and ISS eligible-countries page. NOT confirmed on minfor.gov.gy.
Mexican ordinary passport holders with a valid Canadian visa may enter visa-free for up to 90 days. Source: Embassy of Guyana Belgium and ISS eligible-countries page. NOT confirmed on minfor.gov.gy.
Mexican ordinary passport holders with a valid Schengen visa may enter visa-free for up to 90 days. Source: Embassy of Guyana Belgium and ISS eligible-countries page. NOT confirmed on minfor.gov.gy.

Guatemalan diplomatic and official passport holders enter visa-free for up to 90 days. MFA verbatim: 'Guatemala for 90 days for holders of Diplomatic/Official Passports and holders of an US Permanent Resident Card or a valid US/Canadian/Schengen Visa'.

Cuban diplomatic, official and service passport holders enter visa-free. MFA verbatim: 'Cuba for holders of Diplomatic/Official/Service Passports'. Note: ISS eligible-countries page lists Cuba without restriction - MFA (primary) is authoritative for diplomatic/service/official restriction.
Venezuelan diplomatic, official and service passport holders enter visa-free. MFA verbatim: 'Venezuela for holders of Diplomatic/Official/Service Passports'.
Bolivian diplomatic, service and official passport holders enter visa-free. MFA verbatim: 'Bolivia for holders of Diplomatic/Service/Official Passports'.

Uruguayan diplomatic and official passport holders enter visa-free for up to 90 days. MFA verbatim: 'Uruguay for holders of Diplomatic/Official Passports for 90 days'. Note: ISS eligible-countries page says ordinary passports also eligible for 90 days - MFA (primary) restricts to diplomatic/official; ordinary-passport visa-free access is NOT confirmed on minfor.gov.gy.

Haitian diplomatic passport holders only. MFA verbatim: 'Haitians for holders of Diplomatic Passport'. Note: ISS page says 90 days for diplomatic/official/special - MFA says diplomatic only with no stay limit; MFA used.
Chinese diplomatic and service passport holders enter visa-free. MFA verbatim: 'China (People's Republic of) for holders of Diplomatic/Service Passports'.
Cypriot diplomatic and service passport holders enter visa-free. MFA verbatim: 'Cyprus for holders of Diplomatic/Service Passports'.

Indonesian diplomatic and service passport holders enter visa-free for up to 30 days. MFA verbatim: 'Indonesia for 30 days for holders of Diplomatic/Service Passports'. Note: ISS eligible-countries page lists Indonesia at 30 days without a passport-type restriction - MFA (primary) restricts to diplomatic/service passports only.
Israeli diplomatic and service passport holders enter visa-free. MFA verbatim: 'Israel for holders of Diplomatic/Service Passports'.

Indian diplomatic, official and service passport holders enter visa-free. MFA verbatim: 'India for holders of Diplomatic/Official/Service Passports'. OCI cardholders are NOT covered - no official Guyana source mentions OCI as a qualifying credential.
Kuwaiti diplomatic and service passport holders enter visa-free for up to 90 days. MFA verbatim: 'Kuwait for holders of Diplomatic/Service Passports for 90 days'.
Turkish diplomatic, service and special passport holders enter visa-free for up to 90 days. MFA verbatim: 'Türkiye for holders of Diplomatic/Service/Special Passports for 90 days'.

Mexican diplomatic, official and service passport holders enter visa-free for up to 90 days (unconditional, by passport type alone). MFA verbatim: 'Mexico for 90 days for holders of Diplomatic/Official/Service Passports'.

Georgian diplomatic passport holders only. MFA verbatim: 'Georgia for holders of Diplomatic Passports'. Note: ISS page says 90 days for diplomatic/official/service - MFA says diplomatic only; MFA used as primary.

Spanish diplomatic passport holders only. MFA verbatim: 'Spain for holders of Diplomatic passports'. Note: ISS eligible-countries page lists Spain without restriction - MFA (primary) restricts to diplomatic passports only.

outbound_official.notes: Guyana Ministry of Foreign Affairs (minfor.gov.gy/visa-information) publishes two lists: (1) who may enter Guyana without a visa, and (2) countries for which Guyanese citizens do not require a visa. The outbound list below is sourced from list (2). Entries restricted to diplomatic/official/service passports only are excluded from visa_free and visa_on_arrival - they apply only to non-ordinary passport holders. Cuba (dipl/official/service only), Bolivia (dipl/service/official only), Venezuela (dipl/official/service only), Uruguay (dipl/official only), Mexico (dipl/official/service only), Guatemala (dipl/official or with US/CA/Schengen visa/PR), China (dipl/service only), Cyprus (dipl/service only), Indonesia (dipl/service only), Israel (dipl/service only), Kuwait (dipl/service only), Turkey (dipl/service/special only), Georgia (dipl only), Spain (dipl only), Switzerland (dipl/service for official short-term events only), India (dipl/official/service only) are NOT listed here as they do not apply to ordinary-passport Guyanese citizens. Page last updated: October 16, 2025.

visa_types.name: Visitor Visa (Tourist)
visa_types.purpose: For tourism, vacation, meetings, workshops, seminars, events, religious functions, site visits, and short-term training.
visa_types.notes: Single-entry valid 3 months (EUR 45 at Belgian embassy). Multiple-entry also available (valid 6 months, EUR 75). Embassy in Belgium does not accept electronic visa - physical submission required for that mission. Online pre-approval application available via eservices.iss.gov.gy for applicants in countries without a Guyana embassy. Processing approx. 5 working days at embassy; 10-14 working days via online portal. Guyana does not issue traditional e-visas - online portal issues a pre-approval letter. Fee shown is EUR 45 converted approximately to USD.

visa_types.name: Visitor Visa (Multiple Entry)
visa_types.purpose: For repeated short-term visits for tourism, vacation, meetings, and similar purposes.
visa_types.notes: Multiple-entry, valid 6 months (EUR 75 at Belgian embassy). Same requirements as single-entry visitor visa. Online pre-approval available via eservices.iss.gov.gy for countries without Guyana diplomatic presence.

visa_types.name: Transit Visa / Landing Permit
visa_types.purpose: For travelers passing through Guyana en route to a third country, or for short transit stays.
visa_types.notes: The Embassy of Guyana in Belgium does not issue transit visas - these are issued by Guyana immigration authorities upon arrival. Whether a transit visa or landing permit is granted depends on the length of stay. Requirements: passport valid 6+ months, valid Yellow Fever certificate (if applicable), proof of onward travel, proof of accommodation and funds if staying overnight. Fee not published on official sources.

visa_types.name: Business Visa
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals conducting business activities in Guyana, including meetings, negotiations, and commercial visits.
visa_types.notes: Multiple-entry, valid 1 year (EUR 150 at Belgian embassy). Requires letter of support from sponsoring company/organization and invitation letter from host company if applicable. Also requires business registration and tax ID certificate from host entity if applicable. Online pre-approval also available via eservices.iss.gov.gy.

visa_types.name: Employment Visa
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals who have secured employment in Guyana and wish to reside and work in the country.
visa_types.notes: Requires a signed contract or letter of employment, police clearance, and medical report. Applications accepted online via eservices.iss.gov.gy. Extensions also available through the same portal. Work permit (landing permit for employment) valid up to 3 years, renewable. Specific fee not published on official government pages.

visa_types.name: Student Visa
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals admitted to an accredited school or educational institution in Guyana.
visa_types.notes: Multiple-entry, valid 3 years (EUR 130 at Belgian embassy). Requires Student Acceptance Letter from an accredited institution. Online applications also accepted via eservices.iss.gov.gy. Extensions available through the ISS eServices portal.

visa_types.name: Visa on Arrival (Pre-Approval Letter)
visa_types.purpose: For nationals of countries that require a visa but have no access to a Guyana embassy or consulate - allows obtaining a pre-approval letter online, which is converted to a visa upon arrival at the airport.
visa_types.notes: Applicants must obtain a pre-approval letter through the online portal before travel - travelers cannot board without this letter. Upon arrival at the airport, the visa is affixed to the passport. Processing time is 10-14 working days. Applicants should confirm receipt with the Department of Immigration and Citizenship at (592) 223-5210. Fee not specified on official sources.

visa_types.name: Landing Permit (Business/Employment/Student)
visa_types.purpose: An in-country permit that allows holders of a relevant visa category to reside and engage in the specific activity (business operations, employment, or study) in Guyana.
visa_types.notes: Landing permits are issued for business, employment, and student purposes and are extensions/supplements to the corresponding visa. Available through eservices.iss.gov.gy as new applications or renewals. Fees and validity not published on official portal.

visa_types.name: CARICOM Free Movement Certificate
visa_types.purpose: Allows qualified skilled nationals of CARICOM member states to live and work in Guyana without a work permit under the CARICOM Single Market and Economy (CSME) framework.
visa_types.notes: Available to CARICOM nationals who qualify as skilled workers: university graduates (bachelor's or equivalent including ACCA), musicians and artistes, sports persons, media workers, registered nurses, trained teachers, artisans with Caribbean Vocational Qualifications, associate degree holders, self-employed and service providers. Fee in Guyana: G$1,500. Processing 3-6 weeks. Participating states: Antigua and Barbuda, Barbados, Belize, Dominica, Grenada, Guyana, Jamaica, St. Lucia, St. Kitts and Nevis, St. Vincent and the Grenadines, Suriname, Trinidad and Tobago. Bahamas, Montserrat, and Haiti are excluded.

cbi.notes: No official Citizenship by Investment program exists in Guyana. No draft law or official program announcement has been found on any government domain. Some aggregator/marketing sites claim a program exists but no official government source confirms this.

programs.name: Employment/Investor Visa
programs.notes: Guyana offers an employment visa for foreign investors/businesspersons. Initial visa can be obtained on arrival; work permit valid up to 3 years, renewable. No minimum investment amount is specified on official government pages. No formal 'golden visa' or dedicated residence-by-investment program exists. Path to permanent residency or citizenship timelines not specified in official materials.

programs.name: CARICOM Free Movement of Skills (CSME)
programs.eligibility: CARICOM nationals who qualify as skilled workers: university graduates (bachelor's or equivalent including ACCA), musicians and artistes, sports persons, media workers, registered nurses, trained teachers, artisans with Caribbean Vocational Qualifications, associate degree holders. Also covers self-employed and service providers.
programs.notes: Under the CARICOM Single Market and Economy (CSME) / Revised Treaty of Chaguaramas. Qualified CARICOM nationals can move and work in Guyana without a work permit. A Free Movement of Skills Certificate is issued. Fee in Guyana: G$1,500.00. Participating nations: Antigua and Barbuda, Barbados, Belize, Dominica, Grenada, Guyana, Jamaica, St. Lucia, St. Kitts and Nevis, St. Vincent and the Grenadines, Suriname, Trinidad and Tobago. (Bahamas, Montserrat, Haiti excluded.)

agreements: CARICOM Single Market and Economy (CSME) - Free Movement of Skilled Nationals
